title: Rate Limiting
description: Understanding API rate limits and how to handle them

content.introduction: The Lobstr.io API enforces rate limits to ensure fair usage and stable service performance. When you exceed the rate limit, the API returns a `429 Too Many Requests` response.

rateLimitHeaders.title: Rate Limit Headers
rateLimitHeaders.content: Every API response includes headers to help you track your rate limit usage:

rows.description: Number of requests remaining in the current rate limit window
UNIX timestamp indicating when the rate limit window resets
Seconds until you can retry after hitting the limit

endpointLimits.title: Endpoint-Specific Limits
endpointLimits.content: Different endpoints have different rate limits based on their resource intensity:

errorResponse.title: Rate Limit Error Response
errorResponse.content:
When you exceed the rate limit, you'll receive a 429 response:

```json
{
  "error": "Rate limit exceeded. Please try again later.",
  "type": "RateLimitExceeded",
  "code": 429
}
```

proTips.content: Monitor the X-RateLimit-Remaining header to proactively slow down requests before hitting the limit.
The /v1/results endpoint has a stricter limit (2 req/sec) due to the data-intensive nature of result retrieval.
Repeatedly hitting rate limits may result in temporary IP blocks. Implement exponential backoff in your code.
Use the Retry-After header value to know exactly when to retry your request.

seo.title: Rate Limiting - lobstr.io API Documentation
seo.description: Learn about lobstr.io API rate limits, response headers, and best practices for handling rate limit errors.
